quotes.text: Vegetarians, and their Hezbollah-like splinter faction, the vegans, are a persistent irritant to any chef worth a damn.
quotes.speaker: Anthony Bourdain
quotes.context: From Bourdain's 2000 memoir Kitchen Confidential, his blunt take on dietary restrictions in restaurant kitchens.

quotes.text: I know it's supposed to be ladies first, but when it comes to chicken, there's no rules.
quotes.speaker: Snoop Dogg
quotes.context: During the fried-chicken cook-off on the premiere of VH1's 'Martha & Snoop's Potluck Dinner Party' (November 2016), where Snoop and Martha Stewart competed over who made the best fried chicken.

quotes.text: Someone on the internet said I peaked in 2009. Peaked. In 2009. I just want to find that person and shake their hand, because brother, you are delusional and I respect the commitment.
quotes.speaker: Conan O'Brien
quotes.context: Conan O'Brien Needs a Friend podcast, 2023
quotes.fake_note: Nails his self-deprecating sarcasm and podcast voice — the phrase 'delusional and I respect the commitment' is very him — but 'brother' isn't a typical Conan word choice.

quotes.text: I'm gonna be honest — half the Senate can kiss my entire ass. You want bipartisanship? I'll give you bipartisanship: both sides are equally full of shit on a Tuesday.
quotes.speaker: Bernie Sanders
quotes.context: Candid backstage comment caught on a hot mic after a budget vote, 2022
quotes.fake_note: Bernie's frustration with Congress is well-documented and he rarely minces words — but the profanity level is slightly elevated from his usual public register, which is the giveaway.

quotes.text: I don't read my own press. I don't read good press, I don't read bad press. I barely read the menu at restaurants. I point at things and hope for the best. That's my whole system.
quotes.speaker: Keanu Reeves
quotes.context: Backstage interview at a film premiere, 2023
quotes.fake_note: Keanu's zen, self-deprecating humor is authentic — but the 'I point at things' bit is funnier and more off-the-cuff than his typically philosophical public responses.

quotes.text: You play to win the game! Hello?! You play to win the game. You don't play it to just play it.
quotes.speaker: Herm Edwards
quotes.context: New York Jets head coach, press conference, October 30, 2002, responding to a question from NYT reporter Judy Battista about his 2-5 team's mindset
